reasoning: The administration has clearly demonstrated the intent and capability to arrest/indict James Comey. Earlier in 2025, they installed an interim U.S. Attorney (Lindsey Halligan) who successfully secured a grand jury indictment against Comey for false statements and obstruction. Although a federal judge recently dismissed this indictment 'without prejudice' due to Halligan's invalid appointment, the DOJ retains the ability to refile the charges immediately with a legally appointed prosecutor. Furthermore, Comey is entangled in a fresh controversy as of mid-May 2025 regarding a seashell Instagram post ('86 47'), which triggered a Secret Service probe and explicit calls for his arrest from top officials like DNI Tulsi Gabbard. Given that the DOJ is highly responsive to the President's priorities (evident from Alina Habba recently filing charges against a Democratic representative as acting U.S. Attorney in NJ), the likelihood of the DOJ refiling charges against Comey and booking him before the July 1, 2025 deadline is substantial. There is also a non-trivial chance he was already formally booked (amounting to an arrest) during the processing of the original Halligan indictment. The extremely short ~1-month timeframe remaining (from May 30 to July 1) is the main factor preventing a higher probability, as bureaucratic or legal delays could push a re-indictment past July.
